title: Key Concepts: environment and trade
description: Check your knowledge on environment and trade during the Viking Age!

assessment_items.question_content:
Why were people willing to trade over long distances in the Post-classical period?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Traveling and transporting goods over long-distances was expensive and even dangerous in this period.

choices.content: Trade was not profitable, but it offered a chance for adventure and travel
By transporting high-value goods to places where these goods were in demand, it was possible to make large profits
Transporting goods was usually cheap and easy, so trade offered easy profits

hints: Luxury goods were often used as status symbols because the ability to own them indicated

assessment_items.question_content:
Trade over long-distances focused on

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Transportation was expensive and dangerous, so this cost had to be considered by traders when deciding what to carry.

assessment_items.question_content:
How did environmental factors impact trade routes?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Geography often influenced which routes were more or less attractive to reach specific destinations.

choices.content: Environmental factors completely determined where traders travelled
Trade relationships were determined by economic factors; environmental factors determined the the specific routes used
Environmental factors did not influence the routes traders used; it was purely based on economic factors

assessment_items.question_content:
Cities helped to make the trading process easier by
[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Cities often took actions to control traders, which could make trading more difficult.

choices.content: bringing many potential buyers and sellers together in a single place
controlling important points along major trade routes
